t_ch: 米沙平时的工作累吗？
t_en: Is your work usually very tiring?

t_ch: 嗯，那些行李还挺重的，有时也会碰上难缠的客人。但我很喜欢这份工作！
t_en: Yeah, those bags can get pretty heavy, and sometimes there are difficult guests. But I really enjoy the job!

t_ch: 之后迎接客人的工作交给你啦！
t_en: In the future, we'll leave the guest-greeting to you!

t_ch: 不要和米沙开这种玩笑，我会当真的…不过我也一直在学习更多的礼仪知识。
t_en: Don't joke around like that with me, I might believe you... But I have actually been working on my knowledge of etiquette.

t_ch: 欢迎远道而来的客人，为他们扫去旅途的辛劳，给他们营造良好的体验，虽然只是微小的工作，但米沙依然觉得十分有意义。
t_en: It's my job to make our guests who've come from afar feel comfortable. It might not seem like much, but it means a lot to me.

t_ch: 更不用说，能从大家那里听到银河各地的故事和见闻……
t_en: And of course, the chance to listen to everyone's stories and adventures from different parts of the Cosmos is just amazing.

t_ch: 真希望有一天，列车停靠站台的时候，我也能站在车门边，迎接永不停下脚步的「无名客」们。
t_en: I really hope that one day I can stand by the door and welcome the ever-wandering "Nameless" onto the Express.

t_ch: 真希望有一天，列车停靠站台的时候，我也能站在车门边，迎接永不停下脚步的「无名客」们。
t_en: I really hope that one day I can stand by the door and welcome the ever-wandering "Nameless" onto the Express.

t_ch: 优美的音乐……
t_en: Nice music...

t_ch: 这些音乐，虽然和匹诺康尼的风格不同，但依然很动听。
t_en: The music here is different from what we have on Penacony, but it's still cool.

t_ch: 想听一些熟悉的歌曲吗？
t_en: Do you want to listen to something you're familiar with?

t_ch: 不不…我喜欢了解陌生的东西。
t_en: No, no... I actually enjoy discovering new things.

t_ch: 我的音乐品味挺好的吧？
t_en: How's my taste in music? Pretty good, right?

t_ch: 很棒！它们虽然风格各异，但都让人沉迷。
t_en: It's awesome! Each song has its own style, but they all draw you in.

t_ch: 这些应该是来自不同世界的音乐吧！米沙听到了风雪和寒冷，也听到了古老和生机。
t_en: This music must be from a different world! I feel the blowing snow, the coldness. It feels ancient.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格确实是一个寒冷的世界。
t_en: Indeed, Belobog is a world of ice and snow.

t_ch: 在寒冷中，嗯，似乎还有一丝执着的温暖。
t_en: There seems to be a hint of warmth in this one, despite the cold.

t_ch: 仙舟确实是一个生机勃勃的世界。
t_en: The Xianzhou is indeed an ancient place, but one full of vitality.

t_ch: 惬意，闲适，古老，有时还有一丝危险和紧张的气息。
t_en: Relaxing, classic... and at moments, a bit tense.

t_ch: 如果能和大家一起去往那些世界，亲眼看到音乐中描绘的景象，那就更好了……
t_en: If only I could journey to those worlds with everyone and see them for real...

t_ch: 我也很期待匹诺康尼的音乐。
t_en: I'm also looking forward to hearing the music of Penacony.

t_ch: 如果{NICKNAME}需要的话，我可以随时为你推荐。
t_en: If you ever want any recommendations, {NICKNAME}, just let me know.

t_ch: 如果能和大家一起去往那些世界，亲眼看到音乐中描绘的景象，那就更好了……
t_en: If only I could journey to those worlds with everyone and see them for real...

t_ch: 大家会喜欢吗……
t_en: Do you think people would mind?

t_ch: 我为列车上的大家带了一些小礼物。
t_en: I've brought along some little gifts for everyone on the Express.

t_ch: 这些怀表，是已经不再流通的款式。我把它们修好了，现在它们走得可精准了。
t_en: These pocket watches are designs that aren't available anymore. I've fixed them up, and they're ticking perfectly.

t_ch: 哇，好经典的款式！
t_en: Nice! What classic designs!

t_ch: 嗯，你们喜欢就好！
t_en: I'm glad you like them!

t_ch: 哇，修了很久吧。
t_en: It must have taken you a long time.

t_ch: 没…没花多少时间！都是我平时空闲的时候做的。
t_en: Not much time at all! Just something I worked on in my free time.

t_ch: 它们滴答滴答的脚步，也许能在漫长的旅途中代替米沙陪伴着大家。
t_en: I'd like to think their steady ticking can be comforting, keeping you company on your long trips.

t_ch: 感谢列车上的大家，米沙已经很久没有这么开心过了！
t_en: I'm so grateful to everyone, I haven't had this much fun in a long time!

t_ch: 米沙也要开开心心的。
t_en: Keep on smiling!

t_ch: 嗯嗯！一想到还有大家在银河里坚持着「开拓」的旅途，我就对未来充满了期待！
t_en: I will! Just thinking of all of your trailblazing throughout the Cosmos fills me with hope for the future!

t_ch: 感谢列车上的大家，米沙已经很久没有这么开心过了！
t_en: I'm so grateful to everyone, I haven't had this much fun in a long time!

t_ch: 爱你，太爱你了…
t_en: Lit as fudge! It's just... so peak...

t_ch: 喵…你们列车上的合成机太爱你了。
t_en: Holy shirtballs... The Synthesizer on this Express is lit as fudge.

t_ch: 照这个合成方法，放袋垃圾进去是不是也能变成宝贝？
t_en: Following this synthesizing method, would it turn a bag of trash into treasure?

t_ch: 爱你…这是什么新词吗？
t_en: Lit as fudge... Is this some new term?

t_ch: 不算什么新词，就是特别厉害，特别爱你的意思。
t_en: It's not a new term. It just means "very peak." It means "very fudging awesome."

t_ch: 宝了个贝的…这联觉信标明知道我语言匮乏，还要不停更新词库，真没劲。
t_en: What the fork... This Synesthesia Beacon obviously knows about my language deficiency, but it still keeps updating the lexicon... Fudge this.

t_ch: 可能会变成催吐剂。
t_en: It might become a vomit-inducing agent.

t_ch: 哈？催吐！他宝贝的，原来是剂猛药啊…
t_en: Huh? Vomit-inducing!? Holy forkeroni. So, it's a powerful drug...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能把列车上的垃圾借我合几个吗？偶尔被子弹屑卡住喉咙的时候可能用得上。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can I borrow some trash from the Express to synthesize? Might come in handy when bullets get stuck in my throat.

t_ch: 话说回来，列车上除了这个合成机，还有没有更新奇的玩意儿？
t_en: Anyway, other than this Synthesizer, are there any other cool toys on the Express?

t_ch: 智库播报留声机，统统都能介绍
t_en: The data bank, the Phonograph... I'll give you a tour!

t_ch: 哈哈！看来你这是已经做好当列车向导的准备了啊！那就不多等了，开始吧！
t_en: Haha! Looks like you're prepared to be the Express' tour guide! Then let's not waste any more time, and get started!

t_ch: 哈哈！看来你这是已经做好当列车向导的准备了啊！那就不多等了，开始吧！
t_en: Haha! Looks like you're prepared to be the Express' tour guide! Then let's not waste any more time, and get started!

t_ch: 咳咳，咳咳咳！！
t_en: *cough* *cough* *cough*

t_ch: 咳咳！咳咳咳！
t_en: *cough* *cough* *cough*

t_ch: 很久没喝过味道这么烈的饮料了，比烟熏味的麦芽果汁还刺激！
t_en: Haven't had such a strong-tasting drink in a long time. It's more intense than a smoke-flavored malt juice!

t_ch: 难道是…咖…啡……
t_en: Could it be... co... ffee...

t_ch: 对，就是那位姬子女士的特调咖啡！
t_en: Yes, this coffee was specially brewed by Miss Himeko!

t_ch: 一口下去，苦味火辣辣的，喉咙还会发麻，比嚼子弹过瘾多了！
t_en: It's bitter and spicy, and it even makes my throat numb. It's more addicting than chewing bullets!

t_ch: 姬子特调，好喝又上头！
t_en: Himeko's special brew. It's delicious and goes straight to the head!

t_ch: 是啊，尤其是那种特别的苦味，火辣辣的，让喉咙发麻。
t_en: That's right. Especially that bitter taste, the spiciness, and the way it makes your throat numb.

t_ch: 可惜胃里塞不下太多东西了，不然我还能再来几杯。
t_en: Too bad I can't put too much in my tummy, or else I'd have a few more cups.

t_ch: 很多人说咖啡提神。所以有段时间，我每解决一个小可爱，就会找个地方来几杯振奋振奋精神，好迎接下一个。
t_en: Many people say coffee keeps you awake. So for a while, whenever I had to take care of a lil' fudgehead, I'd look for a spot and have a few cups to keep my energy up for the next one.

t_ch: 可那些小喽啰实在太弱了！咖啡的作用没发挥成，反倒是每天晚上睡不着觉，持续了好一段时间。
t_en: But those little thugs were too weak! The coffee didn't work as intended, and kept me up all night instead. This lasted for quite a while.

t_ch: 今晚也准备好失眠吧！
t_en: Prepare to lose sleep tonight too!

t_ch: 干瞪眼的感觉可不好受。要不…今晚我们过招到天亮，怎么样？
t_en: I hate staring into space at night. How about... tonight we spar until daybreak. What do you think?

t_ch: 咖啡这种东西果然不能多喝，看来得经历一个不眠夜了。
t_en: Coffee is not something you should drink too much of. Looks like it'll be another sleepless night.

t_ch: 干瞪眼的感觉可不好受。要不…今晚我们过招到天亮，怎么样？
t_en: I hate staring into space at night. How about... tonight we spar until daybreak. What do you think?

t_ch: 你相信直觉吗？
t_en: Do you believe in intuition?

t_ch: 你相信直觉吗？
t_en: Do you believe in intuition?

t_ch: 很多时候我都相信自己的亲眼所见，但现在我有点怀疑。
t_en: Most of the time, I believe what I see, but right now, I'm doubting myself.

t_ch: 你是最棒的！
t_en: Don't. You're the best!

t_ch: 很久没受过这样的夸奖了。但我想说的是——那边的角落里是不是放过什么带怨气的东西？
t_en: It's been a long time since I've received such a compliment. What I wanted to say was — was there once something resentful in that corner?

t_ch: 一道意义不明的凝视…有人在盯着我——不是你们的列车长。
t_en: A mysterious gaze... Someone's staring at me, and it's not your conductor.

t_ch: 别怀疑，请相信。
t_en: Don't doubt it. Believe in your intuition.

t_ch: 信归信，但有些感觉靠自信可能解释不清。
t_en: Belief is one thing, but relying on it won't help explain things.

t_ch: 就好比现在，我感觉那边的角落充满怨气，还有一道意义不明的凝视。
t_en: Just like right now, I feel like that corner is filled with resentment, and there's a mysterious gaze upon me.

t_ch: 他宝贝的！我难道中邪了？总觉得这股怨气越来越重…也不知道是什么仇什么怨，有问题打一架不就行了！
t_en: Holy fudgin' shirtballs! Am I possessed? It feels like the resentment is getting stronger... I don't even know what this grudge is about, but if there's a problem, then let's just fight!

t_ch: 不知道丹恒兄弟懂不懂玄学，要不找他来调查调查？
t_en: Maybe Dan Heng knows about paranormal stuff. Should we get him to check it out?

t_ch: 不知道丹恒兄弟懂不懂玄学，要不找他来调查调查？
t_en: Maybe Dan Heng knows about paranormal stuff. Should we get him to check it out?

t_ch: 列车上有螺丝吗？
t_en: Are there screws on the Express?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，列车上有螺丝吗？最普通的那种就行。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are there any screws on the Express? The most common ones will do.

t_ch: 我去找找。
t_en: I'll go look for some.

t_ch: 谢了。都怪这不争气的手腕突然「脱臼」。要是不赶紧上螺丝，一会儿整只手掉下来可能会有点惊悚。
t_en: Thanks. This useless wrist suddenly "dislocated." I'd better screw it back on quick, before the whole hand falls off and gives you a scare.

t_ch: 如果有螺丝刀就更好了，我可以把全身的螺丝拧一拧。
t_en: Even better if there's a screwdriver, so I can tighten all the screws on my body.

t_ch: 你的手腕…！
t_en: Your wrist...!

t_ch: 如你所见，它「脱臼」了。要是不赶紧上螺丝，一会儿整只手掉下来可能会有点惊悚。
t_en: As you can see, it "dislocated." I gotta screw it back on quick, before the whole hand falls off and gives you a scare.

t_ch: 你要不忙，就帮我去找一找。对了，顺便再帮我带把螺丝刀，我可以把全身的螺丝拧一拧。
t_en: If you're not busy, please help me look for some screws. Oh yeah, and if you can also get me a screwdriver, I can tighten all the screws on my body.

t_ch: 这副改造的身体其实挺耐用的，很少掉链子。可能是列车太安全，让它放松了戒备。
t_en: This cyborg body is actually pretty sturdy — it rarely breaks down. Maybe the Express is too safe, so it let its guard down.

t_ch: 不过「检修身体」，也算是一个改造人的必修课了。
t_en: But body maintenance is a required class for cyborgs.

t_ch: 这副改造的身体其实挺耐用的，很少掉链子。可能是列车太安全，让它放松了戒备。
t_en: This cyborg body is actually pretty sturdy — it rarely breaks down. Maybe the Express is too safe, so it let its guard down.

t_ch: 不过「检修身体」，也算是一个改造人的必修课了。
t_en: But body maintenance is a required class for cyborgs.

t_ch: 彻底绕进去了…
t_en: My mind's a complete jumble...

t_ch: 彻底绕进去了……
t_en: My mind's a complete jumble...

t_ch: 惩治恶人难道不算正义？
t_en: Does punishing evildoers not count as justice?

t_ch: 惩恶扬善，纯美的行为。
t_en: Punishing evil is an act of Beauty.

t_ch: 这话怎么听着这么熟悉……
t_en: Why does that sound so familiar...

t_ch: 那要是以暴制暴，算不算正义？
t_en: Does fighting violence with violence count as justice?

t_ch: 当然算！恶人该治！
t_en: Of course it counts! Evildoers must be punished!

t_ch: 如果不只是治治呢？比如打断他的腿脚……
t_en: What counts as "punishment"? What if I break their legs...

t_ch: 或者直接让那小宝贝入土为安之类的！
t_en: Or just put them lil' fudgeheads in the ground directly, you know!

t_ch: 说实话，这种费脑子的哲理我根本就不在意。但谁让我那天被一个小孩儿讲了半天道理呢？
t_en: To be honest, I don't care for this kind of mind-numbing philosophy. But the other day, I got lectured by a little kid about this!

t_ch: 伸张正义要考虑方法，子弹不能打中要害、不能搜刮恶人财产…还高低得给他改过自新的机会！
t_en: Apparently you have to consider your methods when upholding justice. Bullets can't hit vital parts, you can't steal criminals' property... and you have to give them a chance to repent!

t_ch: 这些我都做到了！
t_en: I've done all of that!

t_ch: 小孩子的世界比较纯净。
t_en: The world of a child is so pure.

t_ch: 嗐！做人要有底线，这我赞同。但对于惩恶，一击毙命才是最好的收场。
t_en: Hah! I agree, everyone should have a bottom line. But when it comes to evil, the best way to end it is a single fatal blow.

t_ch: 这些束手束脚的「道德准则」，还是让内心纯净的人去遵守吧！
t_en: I'll leave these restrictive "moral principles" for the pure-hearted folk!

t_ch: 对于惩恶，一击毙命才是最好的收场。
t_en: When it comes to evil, the best way to end it is a single fatal blow.

t_ch: 这些束手束脚的「道德准则」，还是让内心纯净的人去遵守吧！
t_en: I'll leave these restrictive "moral principles" for the pure-hearted folk!

t_ch: 你觉得这几盒子弹…
t_en: About these boxes of bullets...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得这几盒子弹怎么样？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you think of these boxes of bullets?

t_ch: 挺好的，很硬核。
t_en: Excellent. Very hardcore.

t_ch: 哈！喜欢就好，送你了！
t_en: Ha! Glad you like them. I'll give them to you!

t_ch: 可以…嚼？
t_en: You can... chew on them?

t_ch: 你要是有我这口钢牙，随便嚼！
t_en: If you have steel teeth like mine, feel free to!

t_ch: 喏，送你了，保不准什么时候能用上。
t_en: Here, they're for you. Maybe they'll come in handy sometime.

t_ch: 哦对了，还有这个。
t_en: Oh yeah, there's this too.

t_ch: 听说你在找这种彩色「石头」，路过旅行禁区的时候刚好看到，就顺手带来了。
t_en: I heard you were looking for "stones" of this color. I saw it when I was passing the restricted zone, so I brought it with me.

t_ch: 你看看，对不对味儿？
t_en: Look, is this all right?

t_ch: 很对味儿！欢迎常来列车！
t_en: Perfecto! Feel free to come on the Express anytime!

t_ch: 哈哈，不请自来也行的那种？开个玩笑！放心吧，我是个有原则的人。
t_en: Haha, can I just show up without notice? Just kidding. I do have principles.

t_ch: 既然东西送到，我也该走了。我们有缘再见，朋友！
t_en: Since I've delivered my gift, I should go now. Till we meet again, friend!

t_ch: 走了，有缘再见，朋友！
t_en: I'm going. Till we meet again, friend!

t_ch: 放松的感觉…
t_en: The feeling of relaxation...

t_ch: 放松的感觉…真好啊。
t_en: The feeling of relaxation... is so nice.

t_ch: 自从踏上舞台后，我就格外珍惜这样能短暂停下来的时光。
t_en: Ever since I stepped into the spotlight, I have especially treasured peaceful moments like this.

t_ch: 可以常来放松。
t_en: Feel free to come here to relax.

t_ch: 谢谢你，希望下次来的时候不会打扰到大家。
t_en: Thank you. I hope the next time I come I won't disturb anyone.

t_ch: 暂时忘记烦忧和愁苦吧。
t_en: Forget all your worries and sorrows for now.

t_ch: 谢谢你。列车上的温馨布设和安静氛围，让我感到十分安心。
t_en: Thank you. The cozy furnishings and peaceful atmosphere on the Express put me at ease.

t_ch: 这个角度…刚好能看见一片璀璨的群星，真美啊……
t_en: From this angle... I can see a brilliant river of stars. It's mesmerizing...

t_ch: 小时候不懂得什么是疲倦，到了夜晚也不安生，总拉着哥哥一起数星星，而且越数越精神……
t_en: When I was younger, I didn't know what weariness was. Even at night, I was restless. I'd always drag my brother along to count the stars. The more stars I counted, the more energetic I got...

t_ch: 每到那时，哥哥就会哼摇篮曲给我听。往往半段旋律的时间，我就已经睡着了……
t_en: Whenever that happened, my brother would hum a lullaby for me. Halfway through the song, I would already be asleep...

t_ch: 年少的时光稍纵即逝，但那些灿若繁星的回忆却是成长的礼物，值得被永远珍藏。
t_en: Days of youth are fleeting, but those shining memories are gifts of growing up, and they deserve to be treasured forever.

t_ch: 年少的时光稍纵即逝，但那些灿若繁星的回忆却是成长的礼物，值得被永远珍藏。
t_en: Days of youth are fleeting, but those shining memories are gifts of growing up, and they deserve to be treasured forever.

t_ch: 有股动人心魄的力量。
t_en: It has a breathtaking power.

t_ch: 刚才留声机里放的那首曲子，有股动人心魄的力量。
t_en: The song that played from the Phonograph just now had a breathtaking power.

t_ch: 集结、对抗、燃烧的火和坚定的决心。它们唤醒了沉睡的意志，冲破束缚，迎来新生……
t_en: A fire that builds up, clashes, and burns, and an unwavering determination. They have roused the slumbering will, snapping its fetters to welcome new life...

t_ch: 我也喜欢，不愧是知更鸟小姐！
t_en: Impressive, Miss Robin! I like it too.

t_ch: 请别这么说…能够引起人的共鸣和遐想，这才是音乐的魅力所在。
t_en: There's no need to say that... The beauty of music is its ability to resonate with people and stir up their imaginations.

t_ch: 我也时刻提醒自己：想要拥有完美的舞台，务必要在保持演唱技巧的同时，让听众们感受到歌曲中蕴含的情感和故事。
t_en: I constantly remind myself: If I want to have a perfect performance, I have to let the audience feel the emotions and story of the song as well.

t_ch: 说得好棒！所以是哪首歌？
t_en: Well said! So which song was it?

t_ch: 其实…我也很想知道。但以客人的身份登上列车，随意乱碰物品实在太失礼了。
t_en: Actually... I want to know too. But as a guest on the Express, it would be impolite to touch anything.

t_ch: 能听到如此动人的乐曲，感受其中蕴含的情感和故事，我很满足。
t_en: I'm content with being able to hear such a moving tune and feel the emotions and story behind it.

t_ch: 如果可以…能借用几张列车上的唱片吗？三天…两天就行，或者一天也可以。
t_en: If it's possible... may I borrow a few of the records on the Express? Just for two or three days... or even just one day will do.

t_ch: 我替列车长回答了，没问题！
t_en: I'm answering on behalf of the conductor: No problem!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，非常感谢！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank you very much!

t_ch: 那…我想拥有你的签名！
t_en: Then... can I please get your autograph?

t_ch: 当然，也谢谢{NICKNAME}的喜欢。
t_en: Of course, I'm also very grateful for {NICKNAME}'s support.

t_ch: 优秀的音乐作品就像一面镜子，对比映照，才能及时发现不足。
t_en: An outstanding piece of musical work is just like a mirror. Only through comparison and reflection can shortcomings be found in time.

t_ch: 呼…这么一看，我的努力还远远不够。
t_en: Phew... It looks like I still have a long way to go.

t_ch: 呼…这么一看，我的努力还远远不够。
t_en: Phew... It looks like I still have a long way to go.

t_ch: 玩偶…会说话……
t_en: The doll... can speak...

t_ch: 这只像小兔子一样的玩偶…会说话……
t_en: This doll that looks like a rabbit... can speak...

t_ch: 抱歉…好像惊讶过头了。在梦境之外，我还是第一次见到这么特别的玩偶。
t_en: Sorry... I seem to be overreacting. Outside the Dreamscape, this is my first time seeing such a unique doll.

t_ch: 帕姆是列车长。
t_en: Pom-Pom is the conductor of the Express.

t_ch: 列车…长？
t_en: Conductor... of the Express?

t_ch: 实、实在抱歉！之前只听说列车长样貌可爱，但没想到居然…希望它不会生气。
t_en: I—I'm so sorry! I heard that the conductor was extremely adorable, but I never expected... Hope they're not angry.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能跟我说说帕姆列车长平时喜欢什么吗？我想为刚才的冒失做点什么。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, could you tell me what Pom-Pom likes? I hope to make up for my previous blunder.

t_ch: 喜欢烹饪和大扫除！
t_en: They like cooking and cleaning!

t_ch: 这样啊…我这就托管家帮忙，买一些最新最智能的清洁用具和厨具。
t_en: I see... I'll ask the butler to help buy some of the newest and smartest appliances and utensils.

t_ch: 喜欢我！
t_en: They like me!

t_ch: 这样啊…我这就托管家帮忙，照你的样子订做一个可爱版玩偶。
t_en: I see... I'll ask the butler to help make a cute doll that looks like you.

t_ch: 放心吧！帕姆不会怪你的！
t_en: Don't worry! Pom-Pom won't be mad at you!

t_ch: 如果是这样，我更不能无动于衷了。
t_en: If that's the case, then that's even more reason not to be flippant about it.

t_ch: 刚才看到列车长在给盆栽浇水，它是不是很喜欢绿植？我这就托管家帮忙，尽快送些匹诺康尼特有的绿植过来。
t_en: I saw the conductor watering the bonsai earlier. Do they really like plants? I'll ask the butler to help send over some of Penacony's unique flora as soon as possible.

t_ch: 不行，只送东西不够有诚意…还是去找列车长道个歉吧。
t_en: No no, just giving gifts isn't sincere enough... I should personally apologize to the conductor.

t_ch: 不行，只送东西不够有诚意…还是去找列车长道个歉吧。
t_en: No no, just giving gifts isn't sincere enough... I should personally apologize to the conductor.

t_ch: 假发的款式和颜色…
t_en: The style and color of the wig...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来啦，能帮我挑一款假发吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here. Can you help me pick out a wig?

t_ch: 原本想在下次逛街的时候用。但款式和颜色太多，我实在有些纠结。
t_en: I plan to wear it the next time I'm out shopping. But there are too many designs and colors... I can't decide.

t_ch: 统统买下，不做选择！
t_en: Get them all! No need to choose!

t_ch: 换作以前，我的确会统统买下。但我最近在网络上看到很多有关整理的文章分享…好像是叫「断舍离」？我也正在研究学习。
t_en: I'd have bought them all if this were the past me, but I've recently seen a lot of articles online about organizing... I think it's called "Detach and Declutter"? I'm trying to learn more about this.

t_ch: 不得不说，清理完那些不必需、不合适的物品后，整个人都变得清爽了很多。
t_en: But I have to say, after clearing out all those unnecessary items, I feel much more refreshed.

t_ch: 我看看…
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 这些假发的图片看起来还不错，就是名字有些难以理解：比如这款「黑长直」，还有这款「妈生蓝」。
t_en: The pictures of these wigs look good, but the names are a bit hard to understand, like this one called "Black Long Straight" and this one called "Motherly Blue."

t_ch: 三月七眼光好，可以问问她。
t_en: March 7th has good taste. You can try asking her.

t_ch: 我相信三月七小姐的眼光。其实，我刚才找她一起挑选过，但她现在似乎比我还纠结……
t_en: I do trust Miss March 7th's taste. Actually, I just tried picking out a few designs with her, but she now seems more conflicted than I am...

t_ch: …不如，就买这款「金橘卷」吧！偏橘的卷发，逛街拍照也很合适。
t_en: ...Why don't I just get this one called "Kumquat Curls"! Curly orange hair, it's perfect for taking pictures and shopping!

t_ch: 每顶假发都能带来不一样的风格，感觉对练习妆容搭配来说也是一项全新的挑战！
t_en: Every wig brings out a different style. It feels like a new challenge every time I try a different do!

t_ch: 在学习「断舍离」前，还是都买了吧~
t_en: I'd better buy them all before I learn how to "Detach and Declutter"!

t_ch: 每顶假发都能带来不一样的风格，感觉对练习妆容搭配来说也是一项全新的挑战！
t_en: Every wig brings out a different style. It feels like a new challenge every time I try a different do!

t_ch: 在学习「断舍离」前，还是都买了吧~
t_en: I'd better buy them all before I learn how to "Detach and Declutter"!

t_ch: 梦境与现实…
t_en: Dreamscape and reality...

t_ch: 如果梦境能治愈一切，那现实…真的就没有意义了吗？
t_en: If the Dreamscape can heal everything, then does reality... not hold any meaning anymore?

t_ch: 没有现实就没有梦境。
t_en: If there's no reality, there can be no Dreamscape.

t_ch: 是啊，不以现实为基础的梦境，是无法筑造下去的。
t_en: That's right. You can't build a Dreamscape that's not anchored to reality.

t_ch: 可即便如此，还是有很多人宁愿永远在梦中沉沦下去。
t_en: Even so, there are still many who would rather be forever immersed in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 梦都是反的。
t_en: Dreams always show the opposite of what will happen.

t_ch: 嗯，梦也有好有坏…因为结果不确定，所以梦也注定无法治愈一切……
t_en: Mm, dreams can be both good and bad... Because the outcome is uncertain, dreams still cannot heal everything...

t_ch: 可即便如此，还是有很多人宁愿永远在梦中沉沦下去……
t_en: Even so, there are still many who would rather be forever immersed in the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 有些人天生贫苦，不懂得什么叫希望；也有些人天生残缺，不懂得什么叫完整……
t_en: There are people born impoverished who don't know what hope is. There are also people born broken who do not know what it's like to be whole...

t_ch: 人生无法重来，但梦境能让他们体会到另一种人生。所以即使会付出代价，他们也会做出这样的选择。
t_en: Life cannot be reset, but the Dreamscape allows them to experience another life. So even if they have to pay a price, they would still make this choice.

t_ch: 我们无法决定人生，但梦境注定还是梦境。对于那些正在坠入深渊的人，我希望尽我所能帮助并治愈他们，让他们能安稳地回到现实……
t_en: We may not be able to decide our lives, but the Dreamscape is doomed to be just a Dreamscape. For those who fall into the abyss, I can only hope to help them heal and return to reality in peace...

t_ch: 我们无法决定人生，但梦境注定还是梦境。对于那些正在坠入深渊的人，我希望尽我所能帮助并治愈他们，让他们能安稳地回到现实……
t_en: We may not be able to decide our lives, but the Dreamscape is doomed to be just a Dreamscape. For those who fall into the abyss, I can only hope to help them heal and return to reality in peace...

t_ch: 准备了一份礼物…
t_en: I prepared a gift...

t_ch: 一直在各个星系中辗转来回，很少有机会能好好看看这浩瀚的宇宙。
t_en: I've been traveling back and forth between various star systems, and I rarely get the opportunity to really see this vast universe.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，谢谢你邀请我登上列车，片刻的自由梦幻而美妙。我准备了一份礼物，希望你能喜欢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, thank you for allowing me to board the Express. This momentary freedom has been a beautiful dream. I've prepared a gift that I hope you'll like.

t_ch: 我想有一天，我们还会在「开拓」的某一站遇见。
t_en: I believe that one day, at some stop on the Trailblaze, we will meet again.

t_ch: 片刻的自由梦幻而美妙，我想有一天，我们还会在「开拓」的某一站遇见。
t_en: This momentary freedom has been a beautiful dream. I believe that one day, at some stop on the Trailblaze, we will meet again.

t_ch: 你终于来啦！
t_en: You're finally here!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，得亏你来了~
t_en: {NICKNAME}, great, you're here!

t_ch: 等等，你是怎么上来的？
t_en: Wait, how did you get in here?

t_ch: 秘密~还好我跑得快，要是再晚一点，你就看不到我准备的烟花了。
t_en: It's a secret — and it's a good thing I got here so fast, otherwise you would have missed the fireworks I prepared.

t_ch: 这次你要炸列车了？！
t_en: Are you going to blow up the Express this time!?

t_ch: 哎呀，我哪有这么不识趣啦~
t_en: Ugh, I'm not that big of a baddie you know...

t_ch: 虽然做了很多「相互保证毁灭」按钮，但也不是见到什么都想炸上天的~
t_en: Although I've made quite a few "Mutually Assured Destruction" Buttons, it's not like I'm going to blow up everything I see...

t_ch: 烟花？哪里？没看到啊？
t_en: Fireworks? Where? I don't see any.

t_ch: 咦？奇怪…我明明按了按钮呀，是哪里出了问题……
t_en: Eh? That's weird... I pressed the button already. I wonder what went wrong...

t_ch: 小灰毛，别露出这么紧张的表情嘛。
t_en: Gray Hair, don't be so nervous.

t_ch: 我只是拆掉了一个「相互保证毁灭」按钮，把它改造成了「烟花漫天」按钮…哎，我是真的想给你看一场烟花！
t_en: I took a "Mutually Assured Destruction" Button and repurposed it into a "Sky Full of Sparkles" Button... I really wanted to give you an epic fireworks show!

t_ch: 但明明试验了这么多次，不应该呀…第一次登上列车，这按钮就掉链子。还好我还带了几根烟花棒！
t_en: I tested it so many times just to make sure... And now that I finally boarded the Express, it doesn't even work. Oh well, luckily, I brought along some sparklers too!

t_ch: 这就点起来~你看！是不是很好看！
t_en: Let's light them up! Look! Aren't they nice!

t_ch: 列车上不能玩火！下车！
t_en: No playing with fire on the Express! Disembark immediately!

t_ch: 走！我带你出去玩！
t_en: Let's go! I'll give you a tour!

t_ch: 快跑！帕姆提着扫帚过来了！
t_en: Run! Pom-Pom is coming after us with a broom!

t_ch: 等等等等，你上哪儿去？别走啊！
t_en: Wait, hold up, where are you going? Don't leave!

t_ch: 等等等等，你上哪儿去？别走啊！
t_en: Wait, hold up, where are you going? Don't leave!

t_ch: 智库还挺有意思的嘛~
t_en: This data bank is quite interesting.

t_ch: 智库还挺有意思的嘛，尤其是关于「悲悼伶人」的描述，太形象了。
t_en: The data bank is very interesting. I love the entry for the Mourning Actors — it's so vivid and full of details.

t_ch: 他们真的从来不笑？
t_en: Is it true that they never smile?

t_ch: 不仅不笑，天天都是苦大仇深，哀哭不止的样子。
t_en: Not only do they not smile, they are also bitter, grumpy, and weep in sorrow all the time.

t_ch: 如果眼泪值钱，我肯定能靠他们成为银河首富！
t_en: If tears were worth money, I could depend on them to become the richest person in the Cosmos!

t_ch: 你遇见过他们？
t_en: You've met them?

t_ch: 何止！有一次我戴着哀嚎面具，混进了他们的剧团。
t_en: Ohhh, I've met them, alright. One time, I wore a weeping mask and snuck into their troupe.

t_ch: 你完全无法想象那里的氛围，我才待了一天，就赶紧溜出去了…真的，那儿连我这种没心没肺的人都受不了！
t_en: You can't even imagine the atmosphere there — even someone as heartless as me got the chills! I only stayed for a day before I couldn't take it anymore and slipped away.

t_ch: 也有可能是因为这种日子，对我来说太重复了……
t_en: For me, living one's days out like that is too repetitive...

t_ch: 我只想找到宇宙中任何一个有乐子的角落，走哪儿看哪儿。如果乐子不够有趣，我会想办法，让它有趣得令人发指~
t_en: I just want to find a happy corner in the universe and drift wherever the wind blows. If it's not fun anymore, I'll find a way to make it fun!

t_ch: 嘿嘿~温情的诉说时间结束！
t_en: Heh heh, storytelling time is over!

t_ch: 这个故事怎么样？我还编了一个恐怖版的，想不想听听？
t_en: How'd you like that story? I even came up with a scary version — wanna hear it?

t_ch: 这个故事怎么样？我还编了一个恐怖版的，想不想听听？
t_en: How'd you like that story? I even came up with a scary version — wanna hear it?

t_ch: 柔软的沙发…
t_en: The sofa is so soft...

t_ch: 我是不是睡了很久？
t_en: Did I sleep for long?

t_ch: 列车的沙发实在太柔软了，睡着之后…连梦都是柔软的。
t_en: The sofas here on the Express are too comfy. After falling asleep... even my dreams are soft and cuddly.

t_ch: 做了什么梦？
t_en: What did you dream of?

t_ch: 各种片段的组合：清爽的雨后森林、青草地和小狐狸…
t_en: All kinds of sights: A lush forest after a cooling rain, green pastures and little foxes...

t_ch: 后来…梦开始褪色、扭曲。再然后，我就醒了。
t_en: And then... the dream started to twirl and fade away. Then, I woke up.

t_ch: 你睡了一整天！
t_en: You slept for the whole day!

t_ch: 真的？没骗我？
t_en: Really? You're not kidding, are you?

t_ch: 不过嘛，如果不是梦里可爱的森林、青草地和小狐狸开始褪色扭曲。我说不定能再睡一整天！
t_en: But, if the cute forest, meadows, and little foxes hadn't started to twirl and fade away, I really might have slept for the whole day!

t_ch: 我以前从来没有做过这样的梦。在梦里，很多时候我都是从一个面具，走向另一个面具……
t_en: I've never had such a dream before. In most dreams, I just go from one mask to another...

t_ch: 但我觉得挺好玩的，因为有些面具的梦能无缝衔接，就跟走迷宫一样。说不定某一天，我就找到终点了呢！
t_en: But that's fun too — the masked dreams connect to each other seamlessly, like you're walking in a maze. Maybe one day, I'll find the way out!

t_ch: 我可能会被吓醒。
t_en: Dreams like that would scare me awake.

t_ch: 等你有空，我带你去本乐子人亲自认证的恐怖圣地体验一下！锻炼锻炼胆量，怎么样？
t_en: When you are free sometime, I'll take you to one of my favorite scary places to test your mettle — what do you say?

t_ch: 我记不住自己的梦。
t_en: I don't remember my dreams.

t_ch: 很正常啦！我有时候也记不住。
t_en: That's normal! I can't remember mine sometimes either.

t_ch: 只有那个面具之梦，一次比一次清晰…仔细想想，这不就是我的人生吗？
t_en: But that dream with the masks, each more vivid than the last... When I think about it, isn't that an allegory of my life?

t_ch: 我不做梦。
t_en: I don't dream.

t_ch: 嗯？不做梦？从来都没做过梦？
t_en: Huh? You don't dream? Ever?

t_ch: 看来你的体质有点特殊啊，应该多在匹诺康尼多停留一下~
t_en: Sounds like an unusual condition you've got there. You should stay a while in Penacony!

t_ch: 哦？又收到了一个「大乐子」消息，我研究研究！
t_en: Oh? I just received another message — lemme see what kind of fun is inside this time!

t_ch: 哦？又收到了一个「大乐子」消息，我研究研究！
t_en: Oh? I just received another message — lemme see what kind of fun is inside this time!

t_ch: 合作对象储备库…
t_en: The Partner Database...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我有一个问题：星穹列车…有兴趣把我放进合作对象储备库里吗~
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have a question: Is the Astral Express... interested in adding me to the Partner Database?

t_ch: 合作对象储备库？
t_en: The Partner Database?

t_ch: 对呀~我先前听到一些消息，说星穹列车有一个专门的合作对象储备库。
t_en: Yeah! I heard some buzz about this special Partner Database on the Astral Express.

t_ch: 所以…是真事吗？
t_en: So... is that a real thing?

t_ch: 真有吗？我不好说…
t_en: Does it really exist? I'm not sure...

t_ch: 这样啊…好可惜。不考虑考虑建一个么？
t_en: I see... That's a pity. Have you considered starting one?

t_ch: 好！
t_en: We'd be keen!

t_ch: 好！
t_en: Sure!

t_ch: 既然你答应了，我信了！
t_en: Since you agreed, I'll take you at your word!

t_ch: 哎，我可是很认真的，万一你们以后有处理不了的事，我随时可以成为你们的助手哦~
t_en: You know, I'm serious about this. What if you guys run into something that you can't handle? I'll be your nifty on-call assistant!

t_ch: 真的是…助手？
t_en: Will you really be an... assistant?

t_ch: 是啊，真的是助手！别这么不放心嘛，我看上去有这么不靠谱吗？
t_en: Yeah! An actual assistant! C'mon, don't be so distrusting — do I look like someone people can't rely on?

t_ch: 乐子人调查乐子事件，我敢打包票，绝对不会出现事故的！
t_en: A fun-seeker investigating fun things in the universe — I guarantee there's no way that could ever go wrong!

t_ch: …好吧，看你这个表情是不会相信我的…没事，我可以明天再来问你一遍！
t_en: ...All right, looks like you don't believe me... That's fine, I'll ask you again tomorrow!

t_ch: …好吧，看你这个表情是不会相信我的…没事，我可以明天再来问你一遍！
t_en: ...All right, looks like you don't believe me... That's fine, I'll ask you again tomorrow!

t_ch: 依据性格打造面具…
t_en: Craft a mask based on a personality type...

t_ch: 最近想根据性格给自己打造一个全新面具，换一副样子玩玩。有没有什么推荐？
t_en: I've been thinking about making a brand-new mask based on some trait. You know, switch it up a little. Any recommendations?

t_ch: 推荐社恐属性。
t_en: I recommend introversion.

t_ch: 那好办，我在面具上写个「勿扰」，避免和人交流就可以了，对吧？
t_en: That's easy. I'll just write a "Do Not Disturb" on the mask and avoid interacting with people, right?

t_ch: 推荐恐怖属性。
t_en: I recommend horror.

t_ch: 龇牙咧嘴，然后来点红色染料，表情越幽怨越好的那种！有趣，太有趣了！
t_en: Fangs bared, then add a little red dye... The more ghastly the expression, the better! Interesting, very interesting!

t_ch: 随便哪个都行。
t_en: Anything will do.

t_ch: 那不如，面具就索性不用设计了，没有五官的面具，没有性格的性格……
t_en: How about forgoing the mask design then? A mask with no facial features, a personality with no personality...

t_ch: 已经开始觉得好玩了！
t_en: I'm already having fun!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你提供的点子都超——极欢愉！我决定也为你打造一个专属面具！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, the ideas you've come up with are suuuuper Elating! I've decided to forge an exclusive mask for you!

t_ch: 样式我已经想好啦，但得先保密！这样下次见面的时候才有惊喜可言！你说对不对？
t_en: I've even thought of the design already, but it's a secret! Then it'll be a surprise the next time we meet! Don't you think so?

t_ch: 我决定也为你打造一个专属面具！
t_en: I've decided to forge an exclusive mask for you!

t_ch: 样式我已经想好啦，但得先保密！这样下次见面的时候才有惊喜可言！你说对不对？
t_en: I've even thought of the design already, but it's a secret! Then it'll be a surprise the next time we meet! Don't you think so?

t_ch: 带了一点小东西。
t_en: I brought a little something.

t_ch: 快来快来！我给你带了一点亮晶晶的小东西，打开盒子看看吧！
t_en: Come quick! I've brought you a shiny little thing. Open up the box and take a look!

t_ch: 好多层盒子啊…
t_en: This box has so many layers...

t_ch: 盒子套盒子，才显得礼物有诚意嘛~
t_en: A box in a box, that's how you know the gift is sincere!

t_ch: 哇！喜欢！
t_en: Whoa! I love it!

t_ch: 哇！好有耐心，居然一层一层打开了！
t_en: Whoa! You're so patient — you really peeled off all the layers!

t_ch: 难道还有别的方式？
t_en: Was there any other way to open it?

t_ch: 有啊~可以叫我帮你打开！
t_en: Yeah? You could've gotten me to open it for you!

t_ch: 为了成功让这十层盒子套起来，它们的尺寸可是经过精心计算，多一点少一点都不行！
t_en: To make sure these ten boxes fit within one another, I had to meticulously measure them — it would've been a bust if I was off by even one millimeter!

t_ch: 礼物嘛，就是要有仪式感才行。
t_en: It's a present. Of course it's gotta look the part.

t_ch: 礼物嘛，就是要有仪式感才行。
t_en: It's a present. Of course it's gotta look the part.

t_ch: 游离的记忆碎片…
t_en: Drifting memory fragments...

t_ch: 你来了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're here, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我看到窗外有一些游离的记忆碎片，打算把它们「打捞」上来。
t_en: I saw some memory fragments drifting outside the window, and I intend to salvage them.

t_ch: 但…列车车窗是封住的。
t_en: But... the Express windows are sealed tight.

t_ch: 真可爱呢。
t_en: How cute.

t_ch: 我所说的「打捞」只需慢慢靠近那些记忆，再驱动忆者的力量提取保存即可。
t_en: By "salvage," I mean to slowly get close to those memories, and then exert my Memokeeper power to retrieve them.

t_ch: 咦？我看不见，在哪里？
t_en: Huh? I can't see them. Where are they?

t_ch: 这些记忆碎片微小如灰尘，构不成忆泡，很难用肉眼看见，甚至只有少数忆者才能感受到。
t_en: These memory fragments are as fine as dust, and can't form memory bubbles. They're hard to see with the naked eye — even most Memokeepers can't sense them.

t_ch: 星海间有太多消逝之物，那些前人寻过的路和被遗忘的往昔，本都应当在宇宙的进程中留下浓墨重彩的一笔。
t_en: There are too many discarded things in the sea of stars. Our predecessors' trodden paths and forgotten pasts should have left behind vivid marks across the universe.

t_ch: 我曾为这些不留痕迹的逝去感到遗憾，幸运的是，后来我们得以感知、发现、收藏。
t_en: I once felt regret for those that were lost without a trace, but fortunately, we were later able to sense, discover, and preserve them.

t_ch: 不知道今日发现的记忆碎片里，会留有怎样的故事呢？
t_en: I wonder what stories have been left behind in the memory fragments that we found today?

t_ch: 我喜欢听故事！
t_en: I love listening to stories!

t_ch: 记忆碎片！合成！
t_en: Memory fragments! Synthesize!

t_ch: 别急，{NICKNAME}。待我将这些碎片中的记忆一一复现，会与你一同分享的。
t_en: Settle down, {NICKNAME}. Allow me to manifest the memories from these fragments, and I'll share them with you.

t_ch: 别急，{NICKNAME}。待我将这些碎片中的记忆一一复现，会与你一同分享的。
t_en: Settle down, {NICKNAME}. Allow me to manifest the memories from these fragments, and I'll share them with you.

t_ch: 列车上的忆者…
t_en: The Memokeeper on the Express...

t_ch: 那位活泼的小姑娘方才同我说了一些关于「你」的事……
t_en: That lively young lady told me some things about you...

t_ch: 谁？三月吗？
t_en: Who? March?

t_ch: 看来你忘记了，列车的碎镜前还有一位活泼的忆者小姑娘。
t_en: Looks like you've forgotten — there is a Memokeeper girl in front of the Express' shattered mirror.

t_ch: 她同我说你在「忘却之庭」打捞记忆的表现十分出色，是难得的天赋型选手。
t_en: She told me that you salvaged the Forgotten Hall's memories with great skill, that you are a rare talent.

t_ch: 我的表现有六十分么？
t_en: Did my performance get at least sixty points?

t_ch: 这个数字有些保守，在那位忆者小姑娘眼中…你这位在「忘却之庭」打捞记忆的天赋型选手，可远远不止这个分数呢~
t_en: You're too modest. To that Memokeeper girl... salvaging the memories in the Forgotten Hall was worth much more than that.

t_ch: 我没做什么坏事吧？
t_en: I didn't do anything bad, did I?

t_ch: 别害怕。在那位忆者小姑娘眼中…你这位在「忘却之庭」打捞记忆的天赋型选手，可帮了忆庭不少忙~
t_en: Don't be afraid. To that Memokeeper girl... salvaging the memories in the Forgotten Hall is of great help to the Garden.

t_ch: 听说你第一次见到她的时候被吓了一跳。忆者以模因的方式游走世间，有时的确会引发他人产生一些无端的联想。
t_en: I heard she gave you quite the fright the first time you met. Memokeepers travel the world the same way as memetic entities do, and sometimes they trigger unnecessary associations.

t_ch: 记得有一次，我在夜晚寻得了一处散落在玫瑰园中的记忆碎片。当我以为四处无人而后现身时，吓晕了一位正准备告白的先生……
t_en: I remember one time when I found memory fragments scattered among a bed of roses. I showed up when I thought no one was around, and almost scared a man to death who was about to confess his love...

t_ch: 我当时也差点被吓晕！
t_en: I was also almost scared to death!

t_ch: 嗯…忆者们也该思考一下有没有更好的现身方式了。
t_en: Hmm... Memokeepers should probably think of a better way to show up.

t_ch: 嗯…忆者们也该思考一下有没有更好的现身方式了。
t_en: Hmm... Memokeepers should probably think of a better way to show up.

t_ch: 温馨的房间…
t_en: A cozy room...

t_ch: 三月小姐方才带我去了她的房间，很温馨的布设，我很喜欢。
t_en: Miss March took me to her room just now. It was quite cozy. I liked it very much.

t_ch: 还有那面充满了列车组快乐回忆的照片墙，看得出三月小姐很喜欢记录生活。
t_en: And that photo wall filled with the Express Crew's happy memories — it's obvious that Miss March loves keeping records of her life.

t_ch: 照片即是回忆！
t_en: Photographs equal memories!

t_ch: 是啊，照片往往记录着一些值得珍藏的回忆。
t_en: Yes, photographs often record very precious memories.

t_ch: 在探寻记忆的过程中，相较语言描述，图像的展现更为生动直观。
t_en: During the course of finding memories, a picture is worth a thousand words.

t_ch: 三月很热爱生活。
t_en: March just loves life that much.

t_ch: 是啊，那些照片不仅记录着旅程中的点点滴滴。对于三月小姐来说，也是弥足珍贵的回忆。
t_en: Yes, those photos are not just ordinary records of her trips. To Miss March, they are dearly precious memories.

t_ch: 可惜，曾经包裹住她的「六相冰」虽然承载着一些不为人知的过往，加之三月小姐对自己的过去没有任何印象…
t_en: It's unfortunate — the Six-Phased Ice that once enveloped her carries its own mysteries, and Miss March has amnesia about her own past...

t_ch: 所以很遗憾，目前我也无法帮助她找回失去的记忆。
t_en: It is a pity that I can't help her retrieve her lost memories.

t_ch: 还是替三月谢谢你！
t_en: I still have to thank you on her behalf!

t_ch: 不用谢，这也算是忆者的「职责」吧。
t_en: No need. This is merely a Memokeeper's duty.

t_ch: 我相信总有一天，她现在的经历会成为独一无二的回忆。
t_en: I trust that one day, her present experiences will become unique memories.

t_ch: 我相信总有一天，她现在的经历会成为独一无二的回忆。
t_en: I trust that one day, her present experiences will become unique memories.

t_ch: 访客登记…
t_en: The guest book...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这本访客登记簿…方便让我看看吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this guest book... May I have a look?

t_ch: 当然，你写过留言。
t_en: Of course. You did write in it.

t_ch: 帕姆说：请随意。
t_en: Pom-Pom says: Be my guest.

t_ch: 登记簿！值得回味！
t_en: The guest book! That brings back memories!

t_ch: 翻看这本登记簿，仿佛正在聆听访客们的故事与心声…嗯，看到了，这是我的留言。
t_en: Flipping through this book is akin to personally listening to the guests' stories and their heartfelt wishes... Yes, here it is. My message.

t_ch: 这一页，可以交给我来记录和绘制么？
t_en: May I have this page to record and draw?

t_ch: 记录和绘制？
t_en: Record and draw?

t_ch: 仅想表达一些对列车的祝福。这是带有「记忆」祝福的特有文字，来自一个遥远的古老星球。
t_en: I only wish to express my blessings for the Express. These are words specially imbued with a blessing of Remembrance — they hail from a distant, ancient planet.

t_ch: 是对列车的祝福？
t_en: Would that be a blessing for the Express?

t_ch: 不愧是你，{NICKNAME}。这是带有「记忆」祝福的特有文字，来自一个遥远的古老星球。
t_en: Impressive, {NICKNAME}. These are words specially imbued with a blessing of Remembrance — they hail from a distant, ancient planet.

t_ch: 若干系统时后，文字会转变为大家都能够读懂的普遍样式。
t_en: After some system hours, the words will turn into a common language that everyone can understand.

t_ch: 与此同时，「记忆」的祝福也会在那一刻降临，加之给诸位，敬请期待这份惊喜吧。
t_en: At the same time, the blessing of Remembrance will fall upon everyone. Please look forward to this surprise.

t_ch: 若干系统时后，文字会转变为大家都能够读懂的普遍样式。
t_en: After some system hours, the words will turn into a common language that everyone can understand.

t_ch: 与此同时，「记忆」的祝福也会在那一刻降临，加之给诸位，敬请期待这份惊喜吧。
t_en: At the same time, the blessing of Remembrance will fall upon everyone. Please look forward to this surprise.

t_ch: 想来一场占卜么？
t_en: How about a divination?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，想来一场占卜，测算一下未来的运势么？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how about a divination to see what your future holds?

t_ch: 不需要记忆也可以吗？
t_en: You don't need any memories?

t_ch: 当然。忆者的占卜方式，可不只有记忆一种哦~
t_en: Of course not. The Memokeepers' divination techniques don't all involve memories.

t_ch: 未来一片光明！
t_en: My future is as bright as the sun!

t_ch: 我相信你的潜质，能有这样开朗豁达的心境实在不可多得。
t_en: I believe in your potential. It is rare for someone to have such a carefree, optimistic disposition.

t_ch: 我最近有点衰…
t_en: I've been feeling a little down on my luck...

t_ch: 逆位有时也会是表象。说不定在风浪过后，会有意外之喜呢。
t_en: Foreboding signs can also be superficial. You may have good fortune after riding out the storm.

t_ch: 那么，请从金色、蓝色、七彩色这三组颜色的牌中抽取与你意念最为符合的一组吧。
t_en: Now, from the gold, blue, and rainbow decks of cards, please pick the deck that fits you best.

t_ch: 记住，你的第一感知，是非常重要的。
t_en: Remember, your first instinct is very important.

t_ch: 第一组，金色。
t_en: The first set, the gold deck.

t_ch: 我看到了星辰的指引。群星的光芒在漆黑的夜空中闪烁，比月亮更为耀眼。魔术师高举奖杯，木桌上是金色的徽章。
t_en: I see the guidance of the stars. Their radiance shines in the jet-black night sky, even more dazzling than the moon. The magician raises the trophy high, and on the wooden table is a Golden Badge.

t_ch: 你的前路很遥远…且充满了未知与不确定。但只需坚定向前，便能得到指引，从而进展会更加迅速，直至抵达想要的未来。
t_en: The path ahead is long... and filled with uncertainty and the unknown. But so long as you forge ahead, you will find direction, and your progress will be swift till you reach the future you desire.

t_ch: 第二组，蓝色。
t_en: The second set, the blue deck.

t_ch: 我看到了书籍和记录，命运之轮正在转动，知识和经验在其中流淌。远处传来了号角的声音，但岔路却让白马犹豫不决。
t_en: I see books and archives. The wheel of fortune is spinning, knowledge and experience swirling within. A horn sounds from afar, but the crossroads cause the white horse to hesitate.

t_ch: 虽然不知道你正面临着怎样的选择，但请相信，知识和经验一定能够有助于你的判断。它们能够让你游刃有余地应对选择结果。
t_en: I don't know what choices you will face, but please have faith that knowledge and experience will surely aid you in your judgment. They will help you adeptly arrive at your decision.

t_ch: 第三组，七彩色。
t_en: The third set, the rainbow deck.

t_ch: 我看到了充满荆棘的花丛，舒适的躺椅。鸟儿在笼中歌唱，厨师奉上可口的茶点，乐师演奏着动人的篇章……
t_en: I see a shrub full of thorns and a comfortable reclining chair. Birds sing in cages, a chef offers delicious treats, a musician plays a moving piece...

t_ch: …虚幻和空想似乎困住了你。若不想原地止步，需强制自己从美好的幻想中脱离出来，回到现实，这样才能找到属于自己的方向。
t_en: ...The illusory and imaginary appear to have you shackled. If you do not wish to stay stagnant, you must force yourself to break free from these fantasies and return to reality — this is the only way you can find your own path.

t_ch: 希望这次占卜能对你有所帮助。不过，运势占卜的结果仅供参考，只能作为当下的建议。可不要让自己陷入思维的怪圈哦~
t_en: I hope this divination was of help to you. Of course, the results of this fortune-telling are merely a reference — nothing more than suggestions for now. Do not become trapped in your own thoughts.

t_ch: 希望这次的占卜能对你有所帮助。不过，运势占卜的结果仅供参考，只能作为当下的建议。可不要让自己陷入思维的怪圈哦~
t_en: I hope this divination was of help to you. Of course, the results of this fortune-telling are merely a reference — nothing more than suggestions for now. Do not become trapped in your own thoughts.

t_ch: 赠礼微薄，请不要介意。
t_en: My gift is meager. I hope you don't mind.

t_ch: 这是为列车组准备的记忆福袋，赠礼微薄，请不要介意。
t_en: This is a memory fortune pouch I prepared for the Express Crew. My gift is meager. I hope you don't mind.

t_ch: 福袋在发光！
t_en: The pouch is glowing!

t_ch: 好精致的福袋！
t_en: What an exquisite gift!

t_ch: 除去助眠的香薰，还有…我想你会喜欢的。
t_en: Apart from the incense that helps you sleep, there's also... something I trust you will like.

t_ch: 好奇！可以现在打开吗？
t_en: I'm curious! Can I open it now?

t_ch: 当然可以，但是要做好很快入睡的准备哦。
t_en: Of course you may, but you'll have to be prepared to fall asleep very quickly.

t_ch: 请再剧透一点！
t_en: Please tell me a little more!

t_ch: 赠礼需要保留一些惊喜，就把它当作生活中的小仪式吧~
t_en: Gifts must have an element of surprise — just treat it as a part of life!

t_ch: 我已经猜到了，我喜欢！
t_en: I've already guessed it, and I like it!

t_ch: 哦？相当聪明呢~不过在打开福袋前，一定要做好入睡的准备哦。
t_en: Oh? I guess you're pretty clever. But before you open the pouch, you'll have to be prepared to fall asleep very quickly.

t_ch: 再次谢谢你的邀请，不久之后，我也要启程了…列车的下一站会是哪里呢？记忆的流向…又将去往何处呢。
t_en: I thank you again for your invitation. Before long, I too must depart... Where is the Express' next stop? The river of memories... where will it flow to next?

t_ch: 再次谢谢你的邀请，不久之后，我也要启程了…列车的下一站会是哪里呢？记忆的流向…又将去往何处呢。
t_en: I thank you again for your invitation. Before long, I too must depart... Where is the Express' next stop? The river of memories... where will it flow to next?

t_ch: 请容许我赞美你…
t_en: Please allow me to sing your praises...

t_ch: 谢谢你的邀请，{NICKNAME}，我们又见面了，请容许我赞美你诚挚而热情的心灵！
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. Thank you for the invitation. Please allow me to praise your sincere and compassionate soul!

t_ch: 是熟悉的感觉…
t_en: How familiar...

t_ch: 请容许我赞美你高尚的品格！
t_en: Please allow me to praise your noble character!

t_ch: 看来我们颇为默契。
t_en: Looks like we understand each other.

t_ch: 还记得那场幻觉之夜吗？在这熟悉的车厢中，丑陋的巨真蛰虫促成了我们真挚的情谊。
t_en: Remember that night of hallucinations? In this very familiar car, the ugly Giant Sting solidified our friendship.

t_ch: 记得，连盆栽都记得。
t_en: Of course I remember, I even remember the plant.

t_ch: 哦！是那盆在不和谐音符下依然茁壮成长的盆栽。
t_en: Oh! You mean that bonsai that even thrives in disharmony.

t_ch: 如此坚韧又具有生命力的美丽植物，它还好吗？
t_en: What a beautiful, resilient plant full of vitality! How is it doing?

t_ch: 伊德莉拉美貌盖世无双！
t_en: Idrila's beauty is peerless!

t_ch: 如此纯美的行为！看来你已彻底为纯美女神伊德莉拉的美名所折服！
t_en: An act of pure beauty! It appears you have been completely converted by the good name of Idrila, the Beauty!

t_ch: 我曾见证你无畏的勇气与不屈的信念，纯美骑士团的大门永远为你敞开。
t_en: I once witnessed your dauntless courage and tenacious will. The gates of the Knights of Beauty are always open to you.

t_ch: 有缘自会再见！
t_en: We shall meet again!

t_ch: 正如我的内心被喜悦所驱动，希世难得号只差一拳距离就追尾了列车。
t_en: Just as my heart is driven by joy, the One and Only was but a fist's distance away when it crashed into the Express' rear.

t_ch: 但这份美丽的惊吓，也正是缘分使然。
t_en: But this beautiful shock was also a result of fate.

t_ch: 再次登上列车与大家相遇，我实在难以用言语表达心中的感受。
t_en: The feeling of boarding the Express and seeing everyone again is simply indescribable.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的挚友。请允许我用骑士的方式，向列车表达真诚的问候！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, my dear friend, please allow me to extend my sincere greetings to the Express in a way that only a knight can!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的挚友。请允许我用骑士的方式，向列车表达真诚的问候！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, my dear friend, please allow me to extend my sincere greetings to the Express in a way that only a knight can!

t_ch: 令人唏嘘的消息。
t_en: What sobering news.

t_ch: 方才与瓦尔特先生一起听了星际和平播报，有一则消息令人唏嘘不已。
t_en: I was listening to the Interastral Peace Broadcast with Mr. Yang just now, and there was a greatly sobering piece of news.

t_ch: 纯美骑士团中的一名单臂骑士，为追寻「纯美」道途而陨落。
t_en: A one-armed knight from the Knights of Beauty passed away in his pursuit of Beauty.

t_ch: 他没有姓名，也没有任何记忆，只凭借那一抹「纯美」心绪的指引不断向前……
t_en: He had no name or memories — he only relied on the guidance of that trace of Beauty to keep moving forward...

t_ch: 你认识这位骑士吗？
t_en: Did you know this knight?

t_ch: 根据播报中的描述，我确定我与他曾有过一面之缘。
t_en: According to the description in the news, I'm certain I once encountered him.

t_ch: 那时，我在宇宙中捕捉到了一丝「纯美」的气息，并循着气息找到了一个充满废墟的大孔洞。还未等我彻底靠近，就被一股力量吸了过去。若不是他及时抛出了绳索，恐怕现在……
t_en: At that time, I had caught the scent of a sliver of Beauty in the universe, and followed that scent to a Macro Void full of ruins. But before I could get close, I was sucked in by a powerful force. If he hadn't tossed me his rope in time, I might've been...

t_ch: 那后来呢，你们没有同行吗？
t_en: What happened later? Did you not travel together?

t_ch: 很遗憾。我虽自心底里产生了「美」的情绪，也打算永远记住这份恩情，但在表达感谢后，我们还是打算分道扬镳。
t_en: Alas, while Beauty was invoked in the depths of my heart and I vowed to remember this kindness forever, after I expressed my gratitude, we still decided to part ways.

t_ch: 纯美骑士们追逐着各自的目标，走向不同的道路，这是命运使然。
t_en: The Knights of Beauty each pursue their own goals, venturing down different paths. That is our destiny.

t_ch: 倘若契机存在，我会毫不犹豫，拼尽全力助他脱离困境。
t_en: If an opportunity had arisen, I would have spared no effort to help him out of his predicament.

t_ch: …抱歉，这名单臂骑士的陨落令我有些失神。
t_en: ...Apologies, this one-armed knight's passing has left me somewhat distraught.

t_ch: 我打算前往他陨落的地方，为他立碑，并献上一枚纯美骑士的勋章。{NICKNAME}，期待下次的相见。
t_en: I intend to head to the location of his passing, erect a monument to him, and confer upon him a medal of the Knights of Beauty. {NICKNAME}, I look forward to our next meeting.

t_ch: 我打算前往他陨落的地方，为他立碑，并献上一枚纯美骑士的勋章。{NICKNAME}，期待下次的相见。
t_en: I intend to head to the location of his passing, erect a monument to him, and confer upon him a medal of the Knights of Beauty. {NICKNAME}, I look forward to our next meeting.

t_ch: 绒球装饰…
t_en: Fluff ball ornament...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是否在观察我手中的绒球？我正打算把它作为肩甲上的装饰。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you looking at the fluff ball in my hand? I was thinking of using it to adorn the armor on my shoulder.

t_ch: 是！哪里能买到呢？
t_en: I was! Where can I buy it?

t_ch: 它并非买卖所得，而是怪物——「凶恶绒球」的战利品。
t_en: It wasn't a purchase, but a prize from a monster — the Ferocious Fluff Ball.

t_ch: 这团绒球可爱而独特，颇具魅力。因此被我置于肩甲上，作为荣誉的象征。
t_en: This fluff ball is cute, unique, and quite charming. That's why I wish to wear it on my armor, as a badge of honor.

t_ch: 这个装饰有些显眼。
t_en: This ornament is pretty eye-catching.

t_ch: 感谢你的夸奖，我的挚友。这团绒球是怪物——「凶恶绒球」的战利品。
t_en: Thank you, my dear friend. This fluff ball is a prize from a monster — the Ferocious Fluff Ball.

t_ch: 它可爱而独特，颇具魅力。我将它置于肩甲上，作为荣誉的象征。
t_en: It is cute, unique, and quite charming. I wish to wear it on my armor, as a badge of honor.

t_ch: 「凶恶绒球」是一团大型圆球毛绒生物，遇到危险时会旋转起来。届时，毛绒会变成尖刺，犹如箭雨，避无可避。
t_en: The Ferocious Fluff Ball is a giant, round fluffy creature that will spin when faced with danger. Its fur then hardens into spikes, like a shower of arrows from which one cannot escape.

t_ch: 无所谓，我会被可爱打败。
t_en: I would be defeated by the cuteness.

t_ch: 宇宙间具有「魅力」的生物很多。在不知晓对方来意的情况下，请务必保持警惕。
t_en: There are many "charming" creatures across the universe. Please keep your guard up if you don't know the other party's intentions.

t_ch: 怎么才能温和地打败它？
t_en: How do you defeat it gently?

t_ch: 「凶恶绒球」旋转起来时，会有黑色光点在绒球中心闪烁。只需找准机会，便能一击斩落。
t_en: When the Ferocious Fluff Ball starts spinning, black spots will shimmer in the center of the fluff ball. You just need the right opportunity to down it with one strike.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是否还在观察绒球装饰？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you still looking at the fluff ball ornament?

t_ch: 它炸开了…
t_en: It exploded...

t_ch: 看来绒球的魅力仅能保存八个系统时，我为此深感遗憾。
t_en: Looks like the fluff ball's charm only lasts eight system hours. I'm deeply saddened.

t_ch: 手心中残留的一丝毛绒，我打算将它保存起来，作为它在这美丽宇宙存在过的证明。
t_en: I intend to save this remaining bit of fluff in my hand — proof that it once existed in this beautiful universe.

t_ch: 手心中残留的一丝毛绒，我打算将它保存起来，作为它在这美丽宇宙存在过的证明。
t_en: I intend to save this remaining bit of fluff in my hand — proof that it once existed in this beautiful universe.

t_ch: 智库的记录…
t_en: Data bank records...

t_ch: 丹恒先生方才带我拜访了列车的智库。
t_en: Mr. Dan Heng showed me the Express' data bank just now.

t_ch: 查阅片刻，我发觉「纯美」正如智库中所写：祂的消失与其诞生一样神秘。
t_en: Upon examining it for a moment, I found that the Beauty was described as such in the data bank: THEIR disappearance and birth are equally mysterious.

t_ch: 我也很神秘。
t_en: I'm mysterious too.

t_ch: 第一天结识你的时候，你也如这般幽默风趣。
t_en: Your sense of humor is just as sharp as when we first met.

t_ch: 有迹可循，问题不大。
t_en: There are clues. It's not a huge problem.

t_ch: 「纯美」无处不在，我会终其一生追逐祂的行迹。
t_en: The Beauty is everywhere, and I will spend my life following THEIR trail.

t_ch: 智库得大更新了。
t_en: The data bank needs a major update.

t_ch: 不知我可否帮得上忙？倘若需要更多有关「纯美」的信息，请随时来找我。
t_en: I wonder if I could be of help? Please seek me out anytime if you require more information about the Beauty.

t_ch: 智库中的记录涉及诸多星球。各类信息纷繁复杂，却能被整理的井井有条，这实在令我感到钦佩。
t_en: The data bank's records touch upon myriad planets. Each category of information is confusingly complex, but they have all been so well-organized — 'tis an admirable feat indeed.

t_ch: 如果能在希世难得号上打造一间智库……
t_en: If I could install a data bank on the One and Only...

t_ch: 直接复制列车智库吧！
t_en: Just copy the Express' data bank!

t_ch: 你就拥有了整个宇宙。
t_en: You would possess the entire universe.

t_ch: 无论何时，你的回答都会超出我的预期……
t_en: Every time, your responses exceed my expectations...

t_ch: 请容我再次赞美你，{NICKNAME}，拥有一个智慧的灵魂和炽热的心——你，真的很美。
t_en: Please allow me to sing your praises once again, {NICKNAME}. With an erudite soul and a blazing heart, you are truly beautiful.

t_ch: 请容我再次赞美你，{NICKNAME}，拥有一个智慧的灵魂和炽热的心——你，真的很美。
t_en: Please allow me to sing your praises once again, {NICKNAME}. With an erudite soul and a blazing heart, you are truly beautiful.

t_ch: 太空竞逐…
t_en: Space racing...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是否听说过太空竞逐？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, have you heard of space racing?

t_ch: 惊险刺激的飞行竞速比拼，能让紧绷的情绪更加放松。
t_en: A thrilling race among the stars can help calm high-strung emotions.

t_ch: 听起来有点危险…
t_en: Sounds a little dangerous...

t_ch: 这类竞速对于刚入门的新手来说，的确有些难以掌握。
t_en: Such races can be quite difficult for beginners to get a handle on.

t_ch: 不过不必担心，竞逐的主办方为新手设计了专门的减速赛道区。
t_en: But do not worry, the races' organizers have designed slower courses for beginners.

t_ch: 类似赛车？
t_en: Is it like car racing?

t_ch: 十分精准的比喻。
t_en: A very precise comparison.

t_ch: 但银河中的赛道受各种因素的影响，稳定性并不比地表赛道安全。
t_en: But a Cosmic racetrack is affected by various factors. It isn't exactly as stable as a terrestrial racetrack.

t_ch: 有趣！想尝试！
t_en: Interesting! I want to try!

t_ch: 你的勇敢和好奇心令人动容。我与希世难得号随时等待你的到来。
t_en: Your courage and curiosity are truly moving. The One and Only and I await your arrival.

t_ch: 太空竞逐是一场勇气与速度的较量，当引擎的动力达到顶峰，激昂的斗志也会被随之点燃。
t_en: Space racing is a battle of bravery and speed. When the engine's power reaches its peak, one's passionate fighting spirit will also be ignited.

t_ch: 下次比赛开办时，{NICKNAME}与列车组如果有兴趣观看，我会给大家寄送入场门票。
t_en: The next time there's a race, if {NICKNAME} and the Express Crew are interested in spectating, I will send everyone admission tickets.

t_ch: 期待未来有一天，我们能有机会同场竞技。
t_en: I hope that one day, we will have the chance to meet on the racetrack.

t_ch: 太空竞逐是一场勇气与速度的较量，当引擎的动力达到顶峰，激昂的斗志也会被随之点燃。
t_en: Space racing is a battle of bravery and speed. When the engine's power reaches its peak, one's passionate fighting spirit will also be ignited.

t_ch: 下次比赛开办时，{NICKNAME}与列车组如果有兴趣观看，我会给大家寄送入场门票。
t_en: The next time there's a race, if {NICKNAME} and the Express Crew are interested in spectating, I will send everyone admission tickets.

t_ch: 期待未来有一天，我们能有机会同场竞技。
t_en: I hope that one day, we will have the chance to meet on the racetrack.

t_ch: 种子和晶石…
t_en: Seeds and Rainbowite...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我有一个疑问百思不得其解。请问你能否判断：这颗绚丽的五彩种子…真的能通过种植得到五彩晶石吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I have a question that I just can't wrap my head around. I would like to ask you: This splendid rainbow seed... Can it really grow into Rainbowite?

t_ch: 恐怕…不行…吧。
t_en: I'm afraid... not... I think.

t_ch: 不行吗…可那位老板说这颗同样绚丽的五彩晶石正是五彩种子的产物。
t_en: Really... But that shopkeeper said that this equally splendid Rainbowite is the product of rainbow seeds.

t_ch: 感觉像被卖家骗了。
t_en: Feels like you were ripped off.

t_ch: 这样吗…我记得那位老板说这颗同样绚丽的五彩晶石正是五彩种子的产物。
t_en: Is that so... I recall that shopkeeper said that this equally splendid Rainbowite is the product of rainbow seeds.

t_ch: 它们如此绚丽多彩，实在难以评判。不知道列车上是否有方法可以鉴别种子和晶石的真伪？
t_en: They glimmer with such brilliance. It's really hard to determine. I wonder if there is some way to appraise the authenticity of the seeds and Rainbowite? Perhaps right here on the Express?

t_ch: 可以找姬子，姬子智慧过人！
t_en: Look for Himeko. Her wisdom is unparalleled!

t_ch: 可以找杨叔，杨叔见多识广！
t_en: Look for Mr. Yang. Mr. Yang is very experienced and knowledgeable!

t_ch: 可以找帕姆，帕姆…可爱！
t_en: Look for Pom-Pom-Pom. Pom-Pom... is cute!

t_ch: 我会潜心求教，避免类似的事件再次发生。
t_en: I will seek advice, to prevent something like this from happening again.

t_ch: 虽然晶石是真，但我为自己的疏忽感到十分惭愧。
t_en: Although the Rainbowite is real, I am extremely ashamed of my negligence.

t_ch: 没想到第一次为友人购置礼品，就出现了这样的失误，万分抱歉。
t_en: I never thought I'd make such an embarrassing blunder when buying a present for a dear friend for the first time. A thousand apologies.

t_ch: 别自责，这份礼物我们都很喜欢！
t_en: Don't beat yourself up. We all love this gift!

t_ch: 别自责，这样的礼物多多益善！
t_en: Don't beat yourself up. With gifts like these, the more the better!

t_ch: 谢谢大家的理解。我打算即刻出发，下一次的礼物，我向各位保证：一定不会辜负「纯美」盛名，出现任何纰漏。
t_en: Thank you all for your understanding. Now, I must take my leave. For my next gift, I promise you this: I will not fail the good name of Beauty.

t_ch: 谢谢大家的理解。我打算即刻出发，下一次的礼物，我向各位保证：一定不会辜负「纯美」盛名，出现任何纰漏。
t_en: Thank you all for your understanding. Now, I must take my leave. For my next gift, I promise you this: I will not fail the good name of Beauty.

t_ch: 好困……
t_en: So sleepy...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我来了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm here.

t_ch: 我昨晚一夜没睡……
t_en: I didn't get a wink of sleep last night.

t_ch: 好…好重的黑眼圈。
t_en: You... you have very dark circles under your eyes.

t_ch: 我来之前照过镜子，明明那时还没有的……
t_en: I looked in the mirror before I came and didn't see any...

t_ch: 喝点姬子的咖啡吧！
t_en: Drink some of Himeko's coffee!

t_ch: 嗯？难不成咖啡还能消黑眼圈吗？怪不得这个味道…闻上去和丹鼎司熬制的汤药一样苦……
t_en: Huh? Can coffee get rid of dark circles under your eyes? No wonder it smells like... medicine brewed by the Alchemy Commission...

t_ch: 喝就不必了，多谢你的好意。
t_en: I appreciate the gesture, but I'll pass.

t_ch: 要不休息一会儿吧？
t_en: Why don't you take a break?

t_ch: 不必了，我不困，真的不困……
t_en: No need, I'm not tired, really...

t_ch: 喝点姬子的咖啡吧！
t_en: Drink some of Himeko's coffee!

t_ch: 我刚刚闻到那个味道的时候，就清醒了…闻上去和丹鼎司熬制的汤药一样苦！
t_en: I just got a whiff of it, and it woke me right up. It smells like medicine brewed by the Alchemy Commission!

t_ch: 喝就不必了，多谢你的好意。
t_en: I appreciate the gesture, but I'll pass.

t_ch: 其实收到你的邀请后，我就一直幻想列车内是什么样子，越想越兴奋，一整晚都没睡。
t_en: Actually, after I got your invitation, I couldn't stop imagining what the Express looked like on the inside. I got so excited about it that I couldn't fall asleep all night.

t_ch: 刚刚在你来之前，我已经东看西看了好一阵子…我自己都觉得不对劲，但是完全停不下来。
t_en: Before you came, I already had a look around... I know it wasn't appropriate, but I just couldn't help myself.

t_ch: 只要你想来，列车随时欢迎你！
t_en: You're free to come whenever you want!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，说话算数！我会常来的！
t_en: You better keep your word {NICKNAME}! I'll be coming often!
